description:
You are a bioinformatics scientist assembling a new chloroplast genome. You need to map the exact boundaries of its classical quadripartite structure before publication. 

The unannotated Arabidopsis thaliana chloroplast genome is located at ~/UGENE_Data/botany/arabidopsis_cp.fasta.

Perform the following structural analysis in UGENE:

1. Open the unannotated chloroplast genome FASTA file in UGENE.

2. Use UGENE's structural analysis tools (e.g., Analyze > Find Inverted Repeats, or the Dotplot tool) to identify the two massive Inverted Repeats (IRb and IRa) which define the quadripartite structure. These repeats are typically ~26,000 bp in length.

3. Identify the exact start and end coordinates of both Inverted Repeats.

4. Create two sequence annotations for these regions. Name both annotations 'Inverted_Repeat' and place them in a new annotation group called 'cp_structure'.

5. Save the newly annotated sequence as a GenBank file to ~/UGENE_Data/botany/results/arabidopsis_cp_annotated.gb

6. Extract the DNA sequence of ONE of the inverted repeats and save it in FASTA format to ~/UGENE_Data/botany/results/IR_sequence.fasta

7. Create a structural summary report at ~/UGENE_Data/botany/results/cp_structure_report.txt containing:
   - The total length of the chloroplast genome
   - The exact start and end coordinates of both IRb and IRa
   - The calculated length of the Large Single Copy (LSC) region (the sequence from position 1 up to the start of the first IR)
   - The calculated length of the Small Single Copy (SSC) region (the sequence situated entirely between the two IRs)

metadata.data_source: NCBI RefSeq NC_000932.1 (Arabidopsis thaliana chloroplast, complete genome)
